name: Optimize better-bash-tool promptGuidelines to maximize cwd parameter usage over cd && patterns

description: Baseline: fixed broken symlink (extension wasn't loading at all), haiku model, 100% cd-free ratio

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement - extension was broken due to stale symlink, now fixed. Need to test with harder scenarios and different models.
asi.finding: The broken symlink at ~/.pi/agent/extensions/better-bash-tool.ts was the ROOT CAUSE - extension never loaded, so models never saw cwd param or promptGuidelines
asi.symlink_fix: ran install.sh which removed stale .ts symlink and created proper directory symlink

description: Baseline with 3-scenario benchmark: 62.5% cd-free (git review scenario uses cd+cwd redundantly)

asi.hypothesis: Baseline with 3-scenario benchmark (git review + dir commands + multi-dir). Extension loads correctly now.
asi.finding: s1 (git review) fails: model uses BOTH cwd AND cd in the command (redundant). s2/s3 pass perfectly. The git scenario is the problematic pattern matching the real session.
asi.next_action_hint: Strengthen promptGuidelines to explicitly address the cd+cwd redundancy pattern, especially for git commands
